metrics.narrative: Anna articulated a clear operational pain on the 26 Jun 2026 demo: the need to move from historical, backward-looking supplier risk analysis to continuous, up-to-date monitoring across a decentralized Global Solutions division experiencing frequent acquisitions. Key qualitative drivers include: (1) bulk upload and automated mapping of acquired companies' supplier data during due diligence so acquisitions' supplier risk can be assessed immediately, (2) combining SRPM (iRisk) with sourcing, contracting, and spend analytics to assess revenue and EBIT at risk by supplier or entity, and (3) addressing data quality challenges and difficulties obtaining data from acquisitions lacking proper ERP systems. However, no quantified ROI, cost savings target, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement was discussed. Anna referenced internal cost-saving initiatives ('show that we are doing cost saving initiatives on a, say, 50% on a yearly basis on the items we are buying') but did not tie a number to this project. Quantifying the value case is the immediate next step; Anna has requested the Value Engineering template to build her internal business case, signaling she needs help structuring the quantified argument.

sources: Avoma call 26 Jun 2026: Anna led the demo, articulated detailed pain and requirements, and engaged deeply on functional fit questions throughout the session.

Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna: 'Request for value-engineering/template to build business case; returned worksheet may require NDA before Anna shares numbers.' — proactive commitment to build the internal case.
Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna: 'Next steps depend on internal budgeting; Anna expects follow-up discussions after summer (September timeframe) to pursue procurement/risk tool procurement and design.'
Next_Step__c: 'Reconnect in September to review budget status, discuss the findings, and plan the next workshop with key stakeholders.' — Anna is driving the next milestone.
OpportunityContactRole: Anna Görtz, role=Evaluator (not Champion or Executive Sponsor in Salesforce).
Single contact; no introductions to other stakeholders yet; budget authority unmapped.
champion.narrative: Anna Görtz (Supplier Assurance Director, Evaluator role) is engaged and proactive but not yet a confirmed champion. Evidence of developing advocacy: (1) she initiated the demo request and articulated clear pain and requirements on 26 Jun 2026, (2) she asked for the Value Engineering template and committed to build an internal business case over the summer, signaling she will carry the project forward internally, and (3) she is planning to reconnect in September to 'review budget status, discuss the findings, and plan the next workshop with key stakeholders,' indicating she will involve others and drive the evaluation forward. However, Anna does not yet have access to the Economic Buyer (budget authority is unconfirmed, and she is seeking approval, not granting it), and there is no evidence she has introduced Zycus to senior leadership, IT, procurement, or finance. She is also the single point of contact; no multi-threading exists. Anna's strength will be confirmed or disconfirmed in September based on: (1) whether she completed the Value Engineering template and secured internal interest, (2) whether she can name and introduce the budget authority, and (3) whether she brings other stakeholders (IT, procurement, senior supply chain leadership) into the next conversation. If she returns in September with momentum and introductions, she is a strong champion; if she returns alone with budget still unclear, she is a coach without power.

sources: Salesforce Competitors__c: blank.

competition.narrative: No competitor named in any source. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is blank, and Anna did not mention evaluating other vendors on the 26 Jun 2026 demo. This is unusual for an enterprise risk/procurement platform evaluation; ASSA ABLOY may be in early exploration (Anna is building a business case before formal vendor comparison), or they may be considering building in-house, using an incumbent system, or doing nothing. The lack of competitive context is a knowledge gap, not a sign we are sole-sourced. The immediate question for the September reconnect is: are you looking at other supplier risk platforms (e.g., Ivalua, SAP Ariba, Coupa Risk Assess, Prevalent, or specialist TPRM tools), have you run a supplier risk initiative before with another vendor, and what is driving the timing of this project (a compliance mandate, an audit finding, a business event)? If ASSA ABLOY runs a formal procurement process, competitors will surface at RFI/RFP. If Anna can direct-source, the wedge is Zycus's integrated S2C+SRPM+AI-assisted onboarding vs point TPRM tools that lack sourcing and spend context.

Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna: 'Combining SRPM (iRisk) with sourcing, contracting and spend analytics will provide stronger analytics, scorecards and the ability to assess revenue/EBIT at risk by supplier or entity.'
identify_pain.narrative: Anna owns supplier risk and assurance for ASSA ABLOY's decentralized Global Solutions division, which is experiencing frequent acquisitions. The specific operational pain articulated on 26 Jun 2026: (1) supplier risk visibility is historical and backward-looking, not continuous and up-to-date, leaving the division blind to emerging risks between periodic reviews; (2) when ASSA ABLOY acquires a company, the acquired entity's supplier data is often trapped in varied ERP formats or missing entirely (acquisitions lacking proper ERP systems), making it difficult to assess supplier risk during due diligence and slowing post-acquisition integration; (3) risk data is fragmented across financial, ESG, cybersecurity, sanctions, and other third-party sources, with no single consolidated view to assess revenue and EBIT at risk by supplier or entity; and (4) in a decentralized organization, generic processes vary across business units, leading to inconsistencies in workflows and onboarding procedures. Anna is the pain owner. The pain is confirmed and well-articulated; the missing piece is quantification (how many acquisitions per year, how much time/cost is spent on manual risk assessment, what revenue/EBIT is at risk today), which the Value Engineering template will capture in September.

sources: Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna: 'Anna need to look at it if I'm we might need an NDA for Anna sending it back' (referring to the Value Engineering template with internal data).

paper_process.narrative: Anna mentioned on 26 Jun 2026 that she 'might need an NDA' before returning the Value Engineering template with internal numbers, signaling that legal/contracting formalities are on her radar and that ASSA ABLOY has standard procurement governance. Casper committed to send the NDA. No other contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec review, procurement channel, signature authority) have been discussed yet. No systems integrator or partner involvement has been mentioned. The paper process is not yet scoped because the deal is pre-budget-approval; once Anna secures budget in September and Zycus advances to commercials, the next conversation must map the contracting path: does ASSA ABLOY use a standard MSA for SaaS, what is the legal review cycle, does InfoSec run a separate review for third-party data integrations (DNB, EcoVadis, etc.), and who has signature authority for a 15k spend?

OpportunityContactRole: Anna Görtz, Supplier Assurance Director, role=Evaluator (not Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor).

Account-level contacts (unengaged on this opp): Marina Lindholm (CFO), Johan Engström (CFO Scandinavia), Stefan Calner (Director Group Supply Management), Hege Yngve Bärgård (Purchasing Director), Ulf Wirkeland (Director Procurement Indirect Sourcing EMEIA).
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. The single contact, Anna Görtz (Supplier Assurance Director, Evaluator role), stated on 26 Jun 2026 that 'next steps depend on internal budgeting' and that she expects 'follow-up discussions after summer (September timeframe) to pursue procurement/risk tool procurement and design,' signaling she does not control the budget decision herself. Anna is building a business case to secure internal approval, which is the action of a requirements-gatherer or project lead, not a budget holder. The account contact list includes CFO Marina Lindholm, CFO Scandinavia Johan Engström, Director Group Supply Management Stefan Calner, Purchasing Director Hege Yngve Bärgård, and Director Procurement Indirect Sourcing EMEIA Ulf Wirkeland — any of whom could be the commercial authority for a 15k S2C/Risk platform spend — but none have been engaged. The immediate move is to ask Anna directly in the September reconnect who will approve the budget and to request an introduction to that person, ideally for a joint session where Anna presents her business case and Zycus supports the commercial conversation.

sources: Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna: 'Next steps depend on internal budgeting; Anna expects follow-up discussions after summer (September timeframe) to pursue procurement/risk tool procurement and design.'

Avoma call 26 Jun 2026: Demo attendees were Anna Görtz (buyer), Nishita Sareen, Casper Hoeholt, Dhruv Mayekar (all Zycus). No senior ASSA ABLOY leadership, no IT, no procurement, no finance — solo exploration by Anna.

Next_Step__c: 'Reconnect in September to review budget status, discuss the findings, and plan the next workshop with key stakeholders' — signals a planned escalation to stakeholders post-business-case, but who those stakeholders are is undefined.
decision_process.narrative: The process is early-stage and budget-gated. Anna stated on 26 Jun 2026 that 'next steps depend on internal budgeting' and she expects 'follow-up discussions after summer (September timeframe) to pursue procurement/risk tool procurement and design,' indicating a planned internal business-case approval step before formal procurement begins. Anna has requested the Value Engineering template to build her case, implying she will present it to a budget authority (likely CFO, Director Group Supply Management, or Purchasing Director) in the September timeframe. No formal RFI, RFP, or multi-vendor evaluation process has been mentioned yet, and no approval-gate structure (single approver vs multi-level sign-off) has been mapped. The demo participant list (Anna only, no senior leadership or IT/InfoSec) signals this is still an individual exploration, not a committee-driven process. The immediate question for September is: who must approve the budget, what is the approval threshold for a 15k spend, and does ASSA ABLOY run a formal procurement process (RFI/RFP, shortlisting) for this category, or can Anna and her sponsor direct-source Zycus if the business case is strong?

decision_criteria.narrative: Anna laid out explicit functional requirements on the 26 Jun 2026 demo, which are the foundation of her evaluation criteria: (1) a central, continuously monitored supplier risk solution (not historical snapshots), (2) bulk upload and automated mapping of acquired companies' supplier data via ERP imports so acquisitions can be risk-assessed during due diligence, (3) risk coverage across financial, ESG, cybersecurity, sanctions/UBO screening, and other third-party data sources integrated into one platform, (4) AI-assisted mapping to transform varied supplier data formats from different ERPs into platform fields to smooth onboarding, (5) combined SRPM+sourcing+contracting+spend analytics to produce consolidated supplier risk scores and assess revenue/EBIT at risk by supplier or entity, and (6) supplier segmentation metrics and quadrants to target improvement actions per segment. Anna also asked detailed questions about third-party integrations (DNB, EcoVadis, Moody's) and whether Zycus contracts directly with those providers or resells access. These are use-case-driven, technical fit criteria; the business-case criteria (ROI, payback, cost vs benefit) are not yet defined and will emerge when Anna completes the Value Engineering template in the September timeframe.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS score on record. The demo content and Anna's use cases signal AI appetite: she explicitly asked about AI-assisted mapping to transform varied supplier data formats and smooth onboarding of acquired companies, and the pain (manual, inconsistent supplier risk assessment across a decentralized division with frequent acquisitions) is a strong AI use case. The Value Engineering template and September reconnect should quantify the time/cost savings AI-driven supplier data mapping and continuous risk monitoring deliver vs manual processes, positioning Merlin as the automation engine for acquisition due diligence and ongoing risk intelligence.

items.change: Opportunity created in Salesforce, stage Qualified, products iSupplier+Merlin+iRisk, amount 15k, close date 26 Jan 2027.
Risk-focused S2C demo with Anna Görtz; strong product fit confirmed; Anna requested Value Engineering template to build business case; NDA may be required; planned September reconnect.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 29 Jun 2026 (1 day ago). Successful risk-focused S2C demo held 26 Jun 2026 (4 days ago) with Anna Görtz, establishing strong product-market fit and a clear next step: Casper to send Value Engineering template and NDA; Anna to build internal business case over summer; reconnect in September 2026 to review budget status and plan next workshop with key stakeholders. Planned 3-month silence through July-August. No other activity logged.

items.detail: 210-day close timeline with zero discovery completed and a planned 3-month silence (Next_Step__c: reconnect in September) compresses the remaining runway to 120 days for demos, RFI/RFP, shortlisting, use-case validation, commercials, workshops, and contracting. If ASSA ABLOY runs a formal procurement process with multiple approval gates, this timeline is aggressive. The discovery call must validate whether 26 Jan 2027 is a genuine business deadline (e.g. fiscal-year budget burn, contract expiration, operational go-live) or an arbitrary placeholder. If arbitrary, re-anchor the close date to a realistic enterprise motion (12-15 months from discovery); if genuine, accelerate discovery and multi-threading immediately to stay on pace.

No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified, and the single contact (Anna, Evaluator) may lack budget authority. If Anna is a requirements-gatherer or project lead without commercial power, the deal stalls at business-case approval unless Zycus reaches the VP Procurement, CFO, or Global Supply Chain owner who controls the 15k spend. The Next_Step__c plan to review budget status in September signals budget authority is unconfirmed. Vulnerability is high that we are working the wrong level of the org.

Single-threaded to one contact with no org-chart visibility, no multi-person buying committee mapped, and no champion with access to power. If Anna leaves, is reassigned, or loses internal sponsorship, the deal dies. Zero redundancy exists.

No discovery call held, no pain articulated, no quantified ROI case, no competitive landscape mapped, and no MEDDPICC coverage. The deal is a Salesforce shell with a product scope and a 7-month timeline but zero validated buyer intent. The first conversation will determine whether this is a real opportunity with urgency and budget or a research inquiry that belongs in a nurture track.

210-day close timeline (26 Jan 2027) with planned 3-month summer silence (July-August, reconnect September) compresses remaining runway to 120 days for discovery depth, multi-threading, budget validation, RFI/RFP, shortlisting, use-case validation, commercials, EB approval, and contracting. If ASSA ABLOY runs a formal procurement process, this timeline is incompatible with a September restart. No compelling event or business deadline stated; the close date appears arbitrary, not business-driven. Realistic enterprise motion is 12-15 months from first discovery, anchoring close in June-July 2027, not January.

Budget authority unmapped. Anna stated 'next steps depend on internal budgeting' and she is building a business case for approval, signaling she does not control the 15k spend. The Economic Buyer (likely CFO Marina Lindholm, Director Group Supply Management Stefan Calner, or Purchasing Director Hege Yngve Bärgård) is unidentified and unengaged. If Anna cannot secure budget approval or introduce the budget authority in September, the deal stalls indefinitely.

Single-threaded to Anna Görtz (Evaluator) with no multi-person buying committee mapped and no champion with access to power. If Anna leaves, is reassigned, or loses internal sponsorship, the deal dies. Zero redundancy exists.

No competitive landscape visibility. Zero competitors named in any source (Salesforce, Avoma, Next_Step). Unknown whether ASSA ABLOY is evaluating other vendors (Ivalua, SAP Ariba, Coupa, Prevalent, specialist TPRM tools), using an incumbent, or considering in-house build. If a competitor surfaces at RFI/RFP and we have no competitive intelligence, we are reactive, not proactive.

Her strength will be tested in the September reconnect: if she returns with budget clarity, a completed Value Engineering case, and introductions to the Economic Buyer and other stakeholders, she is a strong champion; if she returns alone with budget still unclear, she is a coach without power and the deal stalls.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Jun 2026 (1 day ago), demo 26 Jun 2026 (4 days ago), close 26 Jan 2027 (210 days out). Time in Qualified: 1 day. Planned 3-month silence (July-August, reconnect September) leaves 120 days for discovery depth, budget validation, multi-threading, RFI/RFP, shortlisting, use-case demos, commercials, EB approval, contract. A 12-15 month enterprise cycle from first discovery would anchor close in June-July 2027, not January. No forward slip recorded yet; stage and engagement are current, but the date is optimistic by 5-6 months given the motion ahead and the planned gap.

north_star_verdict.headline: The deal had a successful risk-focused demo on 26 Jun 2026 (4 days ago) with strong product-need alignment, but the 26 Jan 2027 close date (210 days out) is fundamentally incompatible with a planned 3-month summer silence until September reconnect, single-threaded engagement, unmapped budget authority, and a full enterprise procurement motion still ahead (RFI/RFP, shortlisting, use-case validation, commercials, EB approval, contracting). The close date is a placeholder, not a business-driven deadline.

flags: MEDDPICC dark: zero coverage on Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Identify Pain, Champion, and Competition. Every element is a knowledge gap.
No champion identified: Anna is marked Evaluator, no evidence of advocacy or access to power, and champion-building plan is absent.

3-month silence planned: Next_Step__c indicates rep will send materials in June and reconnect in September. A 90-day gap from day one on a 210-day close timeline with no discovery completed is structurally risky.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal had a successful demo 4 days ago with strong product fit, but three structural weaknesses put the 26 Jan 2027 close at risk: (1) single-threaded to an Evaluator with unmapped budget authority and a planned 3-month silence, (2) no quantified ROI case yet, and (3) no competitive landscape visibility. The September reconnect is the critical gate: if Anna returns with budget clarity, a completed Value Engineering case, and introductions to senior stakeholders (CFO, Director Supply Management, Purchasing Director), the deal accelerates into formal evaluation; if she returns alone with budget still unclear, the deal stalls and the close date slips 6+ months.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified. Competitive landscape is completely unknown: we do not know whether ASSA ABLOY is replacing an incumbent Source-to-Contract platform, evaluating multiple vendors in parallel, or sole-sourcing Zycus. The discovery call must surface current-state tooling and any active RFP or bake-off. If competing against a major platform (Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) the wedge is Merlin agentic negotiation on supplier price history and iRisk real-time supplier intelligence; if greenfield the wedge is speed to value and procurement category depth.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/Merlin positioning was present but secondary. The demo focused on iRisk (supplier risk monitoring) + iSupplier (S2C) integration, and Anna's key use case — AI-assisted mapping to transform varied supplier data formats from different ERPs into platform fields to smooth onboarding — directly leverages Merlin's AI capability. However, the Merlin/I2O agentic narrative (AI negotiation agents, autonomous sourcing decisions, predictive risk intelligence) was not explicitly featured or differentiated. Anna's questions and the demo notes emphasize functional fit (continuous monitoring, bulk ERP imports, third-party integrations, segmentation) over AI innovation. The September reconnect and Value Engineering case should anchor AI-driven supplier data mapping and predictive risk analytics as the differentiated wedge vs manual TPRM tools, positioning Merlin as the enabler of acquisition due diligence speed and continuous risk intelligence.

items.quote: A central, continuously monitored supplier risk solution is required to move from historical to up-to-date supplier risk visibility across the division.
items.criterion: Continuous, up-to-date supplier risk monitoring (not historical/backward-looking)

items.quote: Bulk upload and automated mapping of acquired companies' supplier data (ERP imports) is needed so acquisitions' supplier risk can be assessed during due diligence.
items.criterion: Bulk upload and automated mapping of acquired companies' supplier data via ERP imports for due diligence

items.quote: Risk coverage must include financial, ESG, cybersecurity, sanctions/UBO screening and other third-party data sources integrated into one platform.
items.criterion: Integrated third-party risk data (financial, ESG, cybersecurity, sanctions/UBO) in one platform

items.quote: AI-assisted mapping to transform varied supplier data formats (from different ERPs) into platform fields to smooth onboarding.
items.criterion: AI-assisted mapping to smooth onboarding of varied supplier data formats from different ERPs

items.quote: Combining SRPM (iRisk) with sourcing, contracting and spend analytics will provide stronger analytics, scorecards and the ability to assess revenue/EBIT at risk by supplier or entity.
items.criterion: Combined SRPM+sourcing+contracting+spend analytics to assess revenue/EBIT at risk by supplier

items.quote: Supplier segmentation metrics and quadrants (e.g., packaging suppliers) to target improvement actions per segment.
items.criterion: Supplier segmentation metrics and quadrants to target improvement actions per segment

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional alignment on the 26 Jun 2026 demo. Zycus iRisk+iSupplier combination directly addresses Anna's articulated needs: continuous supplier risk monitoring (vs historical snapshots), bulk ERP import and AI-assisted mapping for acquisitions, integrated third-party risk data (financial, ESG, cyber, sanctions), and combined SRPM+sourcing analytics to assess revenue/EBIT at risk. Anna confirmed: 'Demo aligns with needs; primary focus is getting timely, continuous supplier risk visibility for the division.' All six decision criteria (monitoring, bulk upload, multi-source risk integration, AI mapping, combined analytics, supplier segmentation) were demonstrated and acknowledged as fit. No misalignment or exposed gap surfaced. The open question is commercial fit (price, ROI, payback), which will be tested when Anna completes the Value Engineering template in September.

hard.next_step: June'26 : Send the Value Engineering template and NDA to Anna to support her internal business case. Reconnect in September to review budget status, discuss the findings, and plan the next workshop with key stakeholders.

pulse.summary: Live: buyer call(s) read this sweep.

deltas.subject: No buyer engagement on record: zero calls, zero tasks, zero LastActivityDate. Discovery has not begun, and the Next_Step__c plan defers the next touch to September, a 3-month gap from creation.

Planned 3-month silence (July-August, reconnect September 2026) with 210 days to close creates a compressed runway. If ASSA ABLOY runs a formal procurement process (RFI, RFP, shortlisting, use-case validation, commercials, EB approval, contracting), the 26 Jan 2027 close is incompatible with a September restart. The September call must validate: (1) is 26 Jan 2027 a genuine business deadline (fiscal budget burn, contract expiration, operational need), or is it an arbitrary placeholder? If genuine, accelerate discovery and multi-threading immediately (no summer gap); if arbitrary, re-anchor the close date to a realistic enterprise motion (12-15 months from discovery → June-July 2027 close) and adjust forecast to Omitted until budget is confirmed.
